query: What is Elon Musk's current net worth according to Bloomberg Billionaires Index in early July 2026? Has it recovered above $1 trillion after the late June 2026 tech selloff? Include recent Tesla and SpaceX share price and valuation trajectory.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Bottom line:** As of early July 2026, Elon Musk **has recovered back above $1 trillion** — but only narrowly. Real-time trackers cross-referencing Bloomberg/Forbes place him around **$1.10 trillion (July 3, 2026)**, with Bloomberg's June figure at ~$1.01T. He remains the world's richest person by a wide margin (Larry Page distant #2 at ~$296B), but sits well below his ~$1.45 trillion all-time peak from mid-June.

**The late-June selloff:**
- Musk became the first verified trillionaire on **June 12, 2026** (SpaceX IPO day), peaking ~$1.45T on June 16.
- SpaceX (SPCX) plunged 16.4% on June 22 and fell >31% from its peak, erasing an estimated $300–350B and briefly dropping Musk **below $1 trillion** multiple times.

**Tesla (TSLA):** $393.45 close (July 2, -7.49% on the day), ~$1.48T market cap, ~21% below its 52-week high. Rallied to $425 on July 1 before the sharp July 2 drop.

**SpaceX (SPCX):** $162.00 close (July 2, +2.83%), ~$2.13T market cap — recovering off its June 25 low of $153 but still ~28% below its $225.64 peak. IPO priced at $135 (~$1.78T); debuted at $160.95 (~$2.1T, briefly topping Tesla and Amazon).

SpaceX's high volatility (~8.7% daily) is the dominant driver of Musk's net-worth swings; he owns ~42% of SpaceX and ~20% of Tesla.

*Caveat: The exact same-day Bloomberg Billionaires Index figure for July 2–3 is paywalled and couldn't be directly confirmed; the ~$1.01T Bloomberg print is from June 2026, while real-time cross-referenced trackers show ~$1.10T.*

key_facts: Bloomberg Billionaires Index listed Musk at ~$1.01 trillion as of June 2026; Forbes at ~$1.1 trillion.
Real-time trackers cross-referencing Bloomberg/Forbes show ~$1.10 trillion as of July 3, 2026 — back above the $1T line.
Musk became the world's first verified trillionaire on June 12, 2026, the day SpaceX (SPCX) IPO'd on Nasdaq.
His net worth peaked at an all-time high of ~$1.45 trillion around June 16, 2026.
Late-June 2026 tech selloff wiped an estimated $300–350 billion; SpaceX fell >31% from its June 16 peak, briefly dropping Musk below $1 trillion.
He has recovered above $1 trillion but remains far below his ~$1.45T peak; net worth dipped under $1T multiple times.
Tesla (TSLA) closed July 2, 2026 at $393.45, down -7.49% on the day, market cap ~$1.48 trillion.
SpaceX (SPCX) closed July 2, 2026 at $162.00 (+2.83%), market cap ~$2.13 trillion, ~28% below its high.
SpaceX IPO priced at $135/share (~$1.78T valuation); debuted at $160.95 close (~$2.1T), the largest IPO in history.
Musk owns ~42% of SpaceX and ~20% of Tesla; SpaceX volatility (~8.7% daily) is the main driver of his net-worth swings.
Musk remains #1 richest globally by a wide margin; Larry Page is a distant second at ~$296 billion.
